quote.msg: 大家覺得入big4做cyber security算唔算有前景
I am now a security analyst in the State.  Here if you have CISSP, you can  get a job very easily.  It can be a way to apply working visa if you can get an employer from US.
巴打 有無咩攻略 要經咩途經apply

replies.msg: In US, CISSP is the single most important cert for cyber security professionals, but nowadays, it is not an easy cert to get as it evolves.  Indeed I would say it is the most difficult test I ever take so far.  Take a 雞精班 if you do have some IT or security background will help.  I took one in HK before.  For others with lesser experience, take the Comptia series program like security+, CySA+ can also help you proceed to the CISSP's level.
Fg留名學野

In US, CISSP is the single most important cert for cyber security professionals, but nowadays, it is not an easy cert to get as it evolves.  Indeed I would say it is the most difficult test I ever take so far.  Take a 雞精班 if you do have some IT or security background will help.  I took one in HK before.  For others with lesser experience, take the Comptia series program like security+, CySA+ can also help you proceed to the CISSP's level.

replies.msg:
cloud 係趨勢唔係must, 佢只係其中一個solution. 如果你話中小企大多數上cloud 我認同，但好多大公司唔會上cloud 係因為有好多confidential data, 所以佢地唔係唔想上，而係唔會上。

quote.msg: 巴打 有無咩攻略 要經咩途經apply

In US, CISSP is the single most important cert for cyber security professionals, but nowadays, it is not an easy cert to get as it evolves.  Indeed I would say it is the most difficult test I ever take so far.  Take a 雞精班 if you do have some IT or security background will help.  I took one in HK before.  For others with lesser experience, take the Comptia series program like security+, CySA+ can also help you proceed to the CISSP's level.
我pass左 ，但pass左點樣apply us d工？

replies.msg: Oh, congratulation!  Do you have any experience in IT security?  I assume you have if you can get CISSP.  Before going into it, I have to say that I did not migrate here via working visa, so my case might not be 100% applicable to yours,  but the fact is that companies here do need IT security people.  Surely they would give priority with someone who has visa already but they would not hesitate to hire you if your background can fit them.     I think the first step is to polish your resume and LinkedIn page.  Resume in US is in many ways different from the one in HK,  I took 6 months to rewrite my resume and got response from agents and employers.  For example, their resume must keep in 1 page, and you have to show your achievement and contributions in your past job.  The agents like  Indeed and Monster would scan resume using AI, so people without the "required" keyword would not even be selected.  LinkedIn is the most effective way to get a job in the State, HR are connected via social networks.  I can show you more if you are interested.

邊個話做infra 唔好做？公司做infra老細可以得閒周圍飛去歐洲開會。平時捉個vendor上黎傾下計又一日，公司上至CIO，下至security team 同app team大佬都要聽佢講野，我都想做佢個位。最慘係佢唔buy cloud，搞到大家冇得玩cloud

不過咁，老細係海歸，開會鬧班鬼佬同大陸佬時可以用極度流利既英文普通話對答。俾著我就唔敢當面屌鬼佬啦

你都on9, 唔用cloud先可以養起infra人
用cloud後最少可以cut一半人

上一份做infra做cloud, 細公司咩都可以自己話事, 啲野行得好快
而家轉咗大公司, 基本上無野要落手做, 全部都搵vendor
但係咩都要等, 成班同事又好似等退休咁, 仲辛苦過自己做
